title: Best VPN for Torrenting 2026: Which Services Allow P2P and Keep You Private
description: The best VPN for torrenting in 2026 must support P2P traffic, have a strict no-logs policy, and ideally offer SOCKS5 proxies. Here are the top options.
content:
Not every VPN allows torrenting. Many providers restrict or throttle P2P traffic to keep their infrastructure clean and avoid legal pressure from copyright holders. If you use a VPN that does not support torrenting, your P2P client will either not connect or connect without VPN protection, exposing your real IP to the swarm.

The essential features for a torrenting VPN are: confirmed P2P server support, a strict no-logs policy that has been audited or proven in practice, a kill switch that cuts internet access if the VPN connection drops, and ideally a SOCKS5 proxy for use directly in qBittorrent or Deluge without routing all traffic through the VPN.

NordVPN is the most popular choice for torrenting. It designates specific P2P-optimized servers, offers a confirmed no-logs policy, and provides SOCKS5 proxy support. Its kill switch works reliably on both Windows and macOS. NordVPN has also passed multiple independent audits of its no-logs infrastructure. The main limitation: the SOCKS5 proxy is only available in the native NordVPN app, not as a direct connection.

Private Internet Access (PIA) has been the traditional recommendation for torrenting because it has the longest track record of actually not logging user activity. PIA's no-logs claim was demonstrated in real-world court cases where it had no data to hand over to investigators. It offers dedicated SOCKS5 proxy servers, port forwarding on some servers (which improves seeding speeds), and a 10-device simultaneous connection limit. At around 2 to 3 dollars per month on long-term plans, it is one of the most affordable premium VPNs.

Mullvad is the most privacy-focused option. It accepts anonymous payment methods including cash and Monero, requires no email address to sign up, and has a confirmed no-logs policy. It does not offer SOCKS5 but its WireGuard implementation is excellent for speed. If anonymity is the primary concern beyond basic P2P support, Mullvad is the right choice.

Surfshark supports P2P on all servers (not just designated ones), which gives more flexibility in server selection. The price is very competitive and it allows unlimited devices. The one limitation for dedicated torrenters: no port forwarding, which limits seeding speed.

What to avoid: free VPNs are almost universally the wrong choice for torrenting. They log activity, often sell data, have bandwidth limits, and the legal protection they offer is minimal. The entire purpose of using a VPN for torrenting is privacy and protection: a free VPN that logs everything provides neither.
